task: Prompt 22: Consent Decree Inventory — Integration Duties Require Inventories

source_documents.role: Core workspace-local research memo on consent-decree / VCA / settlement inventory
Core workspace-local research memo on post-decree implementation overlay
Existing appendix memo used to confirm coding details already compiled locally
Used only to resolve coding gaps already identified in local research (Bridgeport, PRPHA, Los Angeles, Cambridge, Atlanta, Chicopee, ADAPT)
Used to confirm the note’s own framing that Los Angeles, Baltimore, and Puerto Rico decrees enforced distribution mandates

coding_scheme.inventory_requirement: Accessible-unit inventory, count verification, registry, or unit-by-unit accessibility audit/order.
coding_scheme.occupancy_tracking: Tracking who occupies accessible units or, in Olmstead cases, voucher utilization / housing placement / waiting-list / tenant-matching outcomes.
coding_scheme.distribution_dispersal: Express § 8.26 distribution/dispersal term or broader integration/distribution architecture coded in the local memo record.
coding_scheme.public_registry: Public-facing searchable registry, not internal list.

value_legend.yes: Expressly present in local source record
value_legend.no: Not described in local source record

cases.coding_notes: Unit-by-unit accessibility status report across 57 properties.
Accessibility inventory by building, unit type, and feature.

cases.case_name: U.S. v. Quality Built Construction, Inc.
cases.coding_notes: Excerpt confirms ongoing monitoring, but does not expressly mention an inventory order.
Reporting to DOJ on inventory of condominium units made accessible.
Reporting to DOJ on accessibility inventory by building.
Building-by-building accessibility audits required.
Settlement confirms retrofits and compensation, but excerpt does not expressly mention an inventory order.
Accessibility inventory by property with sub-unit feature tracking; reporting table lists 5-year reporting term.

cases.case_name: U.S. v. A.G. Spanos Construction, Inc.
cases.coding_notes: Reporting to DOJ on inventory status by property.

cases.case_name: U.S. v. Bruce J. Menin and Sutton Apartments Co.
cases.coding_notes: Documented inventory of accessibility features.

cases.case_name: U.S. v. Housing Authority of Baltimore City
cases.post_decree_implementation: By August 2015 HABC had completed approximately 702 of the 755 required accessible units.
cases.coding_notes: Internal accessible-unit registry plus reports on tenant placement and accommodation requests.

cases.case_name: U.S. v. City of Los Angeles (Voluntary Compliance Agreement)
cases.post_decree_implementation: HUD had already surveyed 120 developments before imposing the VCA; the AAHR/registry structure remained in place, but compliance lagged badly.
cases.coding_notes: HUD inventory of 120 developments plus searchable registry; supplementary memo describes waiting-list sign-up and policies ensuring accessible units are occupied by those who need them.

cases.case_name: Independent Living Center of Southern California v. City of Los Angeles
cases.post_decree_implementation: As of May 2024, roughly 75% of the term had elapsed and only about one-quarter of the required units had been produced; about 3,000 units remained outstanding.
cases.coding_notes: AAHR database, application/wait-list sign-up, unit classification across 730+ developments, and policies ensuring units are occupied by those who need them.

cases.case_name: U.S. v. Housing Authority of City of Bridgeport
cases.monitoring_duration: Consent order entered Nov. 5, 2019; 5+ years / ongoing
cases.post_decree_implementation: No public compliance monitoring reports were found; HACB reportedly still relies on individual RA forms rather than proactive unit matching.
cases.coding_notes: Supplementary memo provides the key inventory quote; main appendix adds availability, tenant matching, and accommodation-request reporting.

cases.case_name: Puerto Rico Public Housing Administration VCA
cases.post_decree_implementation: Only 74 of 2,560 required accessible units had been certified by July 2016; the December 2021 deadline was missed, and substantial obligations remained outstanding in 2026.
cases.coding_notes: Local memos expressly show unit-creation/distribution targets, disability-needs updates, and reasonable-accommodation documentation, but do not quote a separate inventory/count-verification term.
Settlement is described only as requiring accessibility remediation in senior housing and individual accommodation tracking.
Voucher utilization, housing placement, and housing stability tracking; not an accessible-unit inventory case.
State required to maintain data on placements, unit availability, and integration outcomes.

cases.case_name: U.S. v. New York / DAI v. Paterson (Adult Home Settlement)
cases.coding_notes: Reporting on placements, unit creation, and integration outcomes rather than accessible-unit inventory per se.
Source notes supported-housing obligations but does not spell out a tracking protocol in the excerpt.
Creation of integrated community housing plus placement tracking.

cases.case_name: U.S. v. Delaware / M.A.M.A. v. DeMarco
cases.coding_notes: Quarterly reports on utilization and community-integration outcomes.

cases.forum: Multiple Olmstead settlements (forum not specified in memo excerpt)
cases.coding_notes: Inventory of supported housing and placement tracking, but not an accessible-unit inventory case.

cases.case_name: Philadelphia Housing Authority VCA / Consent Order
cases.coding_notes: Local memo expressly describes accessible-unit vacancy tracking, an accessible-unit waiting list, and quarterly HUD reporting, but does not separately quote an inventory/count-verification term.
Accessible-unit count verification, placement reporting, and accommodation-request tracking.

cases.post_decree_implementation: Completed all 42 required wheelchair-accessible units; final five finished in September 2021 and the VCA was exceeded at Millers River.
cases.coding_notes: Implementation memos confirm a 42-unit accessible-unit production obligation across three developments, but do not quote a separate inventory/count-verification term.

cases.monitoring_duration: 2022 VCA; ongoing / too early to assess
cases.post_decree_implementation: Still too early in the local research record for an implementation assessment.
cases.coding_notes: Supplementary memo confirms tracking and reporting requirements, but the local record does not quote a separate accessible-unit inventory/count-verification term or tenant-level occupancy/RA fields.

cases.case_name: U.S. v. Chicopee Housing Authority and Monica Blazic
cases.post_decree_implementation: Too early to assess in the local implementation memo.
cases.coding_notes: Source memo confirms new accessible-unit production and a disability-rights coordinator, but not a separate inventory or tracking protocol.

cases.forum: Court not specified in local memo (Clearinghouse id 11022)
cases.post_decree_implementation: Local memo treats ADAPT as a rare small-scale success: 248 accessible units required and satisfied by May 2006.
cases.coding_notes: Settlement is included as implementation evidence, but the local memos do not describe an inventory or tracking architecture beyond the unit-production target.

inventory_stat_conservative.denominator_description: Consent decrees/VCAs/settlements with physical accessibility or accessible-unit components; Olmstead voucher/supportive-housing matters coded N/A are excluded.
inventory_stat_conservative.finding: Of 22 consent decrees/VCAs/settlements with accessibility components, 59.1% (13) expressly required accessible-unit inventories, count verification, or registries.

distribution_inventory_stat_strict.denominator_description: Strict express § 8.26 / dispersal cases in the local record (HABC, Bridgeport, PRPHA).
distribution_inventory_stat_strict.finding: Of 3 decrees expressly enforcing § 8.26 distribution/dispersal, 66.7% (2 of 3) expressly required accessible-unit inventories, count verification, or registries.

distribution_occupancy_stat_strict.denominator_description: Strict express § 8.26 / dispersal cases in the local record (HABC, Bridgeport, PRPHA).
distribution_occupancy_stat_strict.finding: Of 3 decrees expressly enforcing § 8.26 distribution/dispersal, 66.7% (2) also required occupancy, tenant-placement, or tenant-matching tracking.

distribution_occupancy_stat_broad.denominator_description: Broader Section 504 integration/distribution set, treating the 2016 Los Angeles settlement and 2019 HUD VCA as separate instruments.
distribution_occupancy_stat_broad.finding: Treating Los Angeles's parallel Section 504 orders as separate instruments, 80.0% (4 of 5) of integration/distribution matters required occupancy or waiting-list tracking.

sensitivity_if_los_angeles_collapsed.finding: Collapsing the 2016 settlement and 2019 VCA into one Los Angeles legal architecture yields 75.0% (3 of 4).

lower_bound_explicit_infrastructure_orde.finding: The coded record expressly shows courts and agencies ordering accessible-unit inventories, count verification, registries, or unit-by-unit audits at least 13 times.

post_decree_implementation_overlay.case_name: Independent Living Center of Southern California v. City of Los Angeles
post_decree_implementation_overlay.details: At roughly 75% of the settlement period, only about 25% of required units had been produced; about 3,000 units remained outstanding; city still lacked an effective database system.

post_decree_implementation_overlay.case_name: Puerto Rico Public Housing Administration VCA
post_decree_implementation_overlay.details: Only 74 of 2,560 required units had been certified by July 2016; the December 2021 deadline was missed; oversight continued into 2026.

post_decree_implementation_overlay.case_name: U.S. v. Housing Authority of City of Bridgeport
post_decree_implementation_overlay.details: Inventory development was required, but no public monitoring reports were found; local memo says HACB still relies on individual RA forms rather than proactive unit matching.
All 42 required accessible units were completed by September 2021, exceeding the requirement at Millers River.
248 accessible units were required by December 2005 and had been delivered by May 2006.

excluded_or_noncoded_items.name: NYCHA informal resolution / compliance reviews
excluded_or_noncoded_items.reason: Section 10 identifies multiple informal monitoring arrangements but not a single public consent decree, VCA, or settlement with enough discrete terms to code case-by-case.

excluded_or_noncoded_items.name: Thompson v. HUD
excluded_or_noncoded_items.reason: Section IV implementation comparator for Baltimore desegregation, but not an accessibility/accessible-unit inventory decree.

notes: The percentages are conservative lower bounds. Underdescribed matters (Quality Built, Design Professionals, Wallace, Chicopee, ADAPT) and inferential inventory cases (Philadelphia PHA, PRPHA, Cambridge, Atlanta) were left as unclear/no rather than inferred upward.

Los Angeles is represented as two separate legal instruments because the local research record distinguishes the 2016 court settlement from the 2019 HUD VCA; collapsing them into one architecture changes the broad distribution/occupancy statistic from 80.0% (4/5) to 75.0% (3/4).

Olmstead matters are retained because they reinforce the same tracking pattern, but they are coded N/A for accessible-unit inventory where the local source record centers vouchers or supported-housing slots rather than accessible units.
